# Back-to-back moves on the same source: the second invocation parses the
# result of the first (fresh hashes, no conflict) and both succeed. Every
# declaration ends up in exactly one file (ADR-0001 sequential case).
exec sflit -source big.go -regex '^Filter' -sink filter.go -move
exec sflit -source big.go -regex '^Render' -sink render.go -move
cmp big.go expected_big.go
cmp filter.go expected_filter.go
cmp render.go expected_render.go

# Sidecar lock files are removed on release; on windows (best-effort
# platform) they are left behind.
[!GOOS:windows] ! exists .big.go.sflit.lock
[GOOS:windows] exists .big.go.sflit.lock

exec gotypecheck big.go filter.go render.go
-- big.go --
package mypkg

func FilterA() int { return 1 }

func RenderA() int { return 2 }

func Keep() int { return 3 }
-- expected_big.go --
package mypkg

func Keep() int { return 3 }
-- expected_filter.go --
package mypkg

func FilterA() int { return 1 }
-- expected_render.go --
package mypkg

func RenderA() int { return 2 }
